title: Breach of Contract - E-commerce Delivery Dispute
description:
On March 10, 2024, Rahul Sharma, a 34-year-old IT professional residing in Andheri West, Mumbai, placed an online order for a high-end smartphone (Model: X-Pro Max, value: ₹89,999) through the e-commerce platform ShopKart India Pvt. Ltd. The order confirmation promised delivery within 3 days, i.e., by March 13, 2024. Rahul made full payment via UPI at the time of purchase.

On March 13, the delivery status was updated as 'Delivered' on the platform. However, Rahul did not receive the package. Upon checking with the building security and neighbors, no delivery attempt was confirmed. Rahul immediately raised a complaint through customer support and email.

The delivery partner, QuickShip Logistics, claimed that the package was handed over to a 'security guard' at the address and produced a digital delivery confirmation with a forged signature. Rahul denied authorizing any such delivery or providing consent to leave the package with a third party.

characters.name: Rahul Sharma
characters.details: 34, IT professional, resident of Andheri West, purchaser of the smartphone

characters.name: ShopKart India Pvt. Ltd.
characters.details: E-commerce company responsible for sale and order fulfillment

characters.name: QuickShip Logistics
characters.details: Third-party logistics provider responsible for last-mile delivery

characters.name: Amit Yadav
characters.details: Employee of QuickShip Logistics who marked the order as delivered

characters.name: Rakesh Singh
characters.details: Security guard of the residential building who denies receiving the package

characters.name: Neha Kapoor
characters.details: Representative of ShopKart who handled Rahul's complaint

evidence: Order confirmation email with delivery promise (dated March 10, 2024)
Platform delivery status screenshot marked as 'Delivered'
Customer complaint emails and chat transcripts
Digital proof of delivery (with disputed signature)
CCTV footage from apartment entrance showing no delivery attempt
Security register logbook (no entry of delivery on March 13)
Legal notice sent by Rahul Sharma (dated March 20, 2024)
Terms and conditions of ShopKart regarding delivery and liability
evidence_chain: Valid contract formed through order placement and payment
Delivery marked complete without actual handover to buyer
Security guard and CCTV evidence contradict delivery claim
Disputed signature indicates possible forgery or negligence
Failure to refund or replace establishes breach of contract
Liability extends to both seller and delivery partner under agency principle
legal_ambiguity: Whether delivery to a third party without explicit consent constitutes valid delivery, and whether the seller can shift liability to the logistics partner despite contractual obligation to the buyer.
